infuse 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • infuse: A Quick Start Guide

    Reap Statistical Inference from Win/Loss Harvest

    reap.Rd

    Calculates point estimates, standard errors, confidence intervals, and p-values for Win/Loss statistics using Influence Functions. It supports both individual outcome analysis and a weighted Non-Prioritized Outcome (NPO) summary.

    reap(iii, conf_level = 0.95, w = NULL)

    Arguments

    iii

    An object of class "harvest" generated by the harvest function.

    conf_level

    Numeric. The confidence level for intervals (default 0.95).

    w

    Numeric vector. Optional weights for calculating the Non-Prioritized Outcome (NPO). Defaults to equal weighting.

    Value

    A tidy data frame summarizing each outcome and the NPO with:

    • Variable: The outcome name (or "NPO").

    • Metric: Favorable, Unfavorable, Net Benefit, Win Ratio, or NNT.

    • Estimate: The point estimate.

    • Se: Standard error derived from Influence Functions.

    • Lower, Upper: Wald-based confidence intervals.

    • PValue: Two-sided p-value against the null hypothesis.

    Details

    The inference for the Win Ratio is performed on the log-scale and then back-transformed to ensure the confidence interval is strictly positive. The NNT is calculated as the ceiling of \(1 / Net Benefit\).

    Examples

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
# Example 1: Survival Data (Ipilimumab)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
data(ipilimumab)
# Higher time is "good" for Progression-Free Survival
fit <- sow(Surv(time, event) ~ arm,
                 data = ipilimumab,
                 treated = "ipilimumab",
                 good = "high")

harvest(fit,lambda = 6) %>% reap()
#>   Variable       Metric    Estimate         Se      Lower      Upper PValue
#> 1     time    Favorable  0.19183870 0.01913764 0.15432962  0.2293478 <0.001
#> 2     time  Unfavorable  0.08507022 0.01201808 0.06151522  0.1086252 <0.001
#> 3     time NetT Benefit  0.10676847 0.02540503 0.05697554  0.1565614 <0.001
#> 4     time    Win Ratio  2.25506284 0.19535996 1.53768701  3.3071154 <0.001
#> 5     time          NNT 10.00000000 0.02540503 7.00000000 18.0000000 <0.001

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Example 2: Continuous Data (Obstetrics)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
data(obstetrics)
# High birthweight is generally considered "good"
fit <- sow(Birthweight ~ Group,
                data = obstetrics,
                treated = "T",
                good = "high")

h <- harvest(fit,lambda = 500) %>% reap()
print(h)
#>      Variable       Metric     Estimate         Se       Lower        Upper
#> 1 Birthweight    Favorable 2.511032e-01 0.01750603  0.21679198   0.28541438
#> 2 Birthweight  Unfavorable 2.456820e-01 0.01721450  0.21194221   0.27942182
#> 3 Birthweight NetT Benefit 5.421164e-03 0.03151462 -0.05634635   0.06718868
#> 4 Birthweight    Win Ratio 1.022066e+00 0.12687684  0.79704178   1.31061944
#> 5 Birthweight          NNT 1.850000e+02 0.03151462 15.00000000 -17.00000000
#>   PValue
#> 1 <0.001
#> 2 <0.001
#> 3  0.863
#> 4  0.863
#> 5  0.863

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Example 3: Longitudinal Binary Data (Respiratory)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
data(respiratory)
# We analyze status at the final visit (Visit 4)
resp_v4 <- respiratory[respiratory$visit == 4, ]
sown_resp <- sow(outcome ~ treat,
                 data = resp_v4,
                 treated = "A",
                 good = "high")

h <- harvest(fit,lambda = 0.5) %>% reap()
print(h)
#>      Variable       Metric     Estimate         Se      Lower        Upper
#> 1 Birthweight    Favorable 5.024753e-01 0.02031357  0.4626614   0.54228915
#> 2 Birthweight  Unfavorable 4.943344e-01 0.02031152  0.4545245   0.53414421
#> 3 Birthweight NetT Benefit 8.140914e-03 0.04062417 -0.0714810   0.08776282
#> 4 Birthweight    Win Ratio 1.016468e+00 0.08151378  0.8663816   1.19255538
#> 5 Birthweight          NNT 1.230000e+02 0.04062417 12.0000000 -13.00000000
#>   PValue
#> 1 <0.001
#> 2 <0.001
#> 3  0.841
#> 4  0.841
#> 5  0.841

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Example 4: Multiple Outcomes (Epilepsy)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
data(epilepsy)
# Looking at seizure rate at Period 4. Lower rate is "good".
epi_v4 <- epilepsy[epilepsy$period == 4, ]
sown_epi <- sow(seizure.rate ~ treatment,
                data = epi_v4,
                treated = "Progabide",
                good = "low")

h <- harvest(fit,lambda = 15) %>% reap()
print(h)
#>      Variable       Metric     Estimate         Se       Lower        Upper
#> 1 Birthweight    Favorable 4.967180e-01 0.02031407  0.45690312   0.53653281
#> 2 Birthweight  Unfavorable 4.884059e-01 0.02030581  0.44860727   0.52820457
#> 3 Birthweight NetT Benefit 8.312044e-03 0.04061376 -0.07128946   0.08791355
#> 4 Birthweight    Win Ratio 1.017019e+00 0.08245985  0.86524480   1.19541554
#> 5 Birthweight          NNT 1.210000e+02 0.04061376 12.00000000 -14.00000000
#>   PValue
#> 1 <0.001
#> 2 <0.001
#> 3  0.838
#> 4  0.838
#> 5  0.838
